_doc: Wire-contract snapshot for the Service-DB HTTP protocol (ADR-0005, docs/service-db-protocol.md). Locks in the exact shape of each endpoint's response body so client + server can't drift silently. The TS types in types.ts and the reference-server (reference-server/public/index.php) both have to keep producing these shapes.

GET /v1/services._doc: Listing with pagination. Container is `items`, NOT `services`. Audit P2 from 2026-05-22: protocol had no test or comment pinning this; if a curator ever switched the key to `services` thinking it was clearer, JS consumers would silently see empty lists.

GET /v1/services/:id._doc: Single service detail. Response is the ServiceMatch object directly (NOT wrapped in `{ item: ... }` or similar).

POST /v1/lookup._doc: Batch lookup. Request body is `{ items: [{ cookie?, origin? }] }`. Response is `{ items: [{ query, matches }] }` — positionally aligned with the request. Each `matches` is an array because multiple library services CAN match the same cookie/origin (e.g. shared FB+Insta cookie names).

GET /v1/health._doc: Liveness + schema version probe. `ok` + `count` are the cross-consumer contract (count optional — when present, lets an external uptime checker detect 'data dropped to zero' even when HTTP 200 says the server is up). A server MAY also emit operational extras (status, serviceCount, lastSyncAt, sourceSha, dataHash) — the reference-server does; consumers must ignore unknown keys, not strip them.
